content.problemStatement:
Chef has a set of distinct positive integers and wants to find the largest subset from this set with a special property: for every pair of integers (a,b)(a, b)(a,b) in the subset, either aaa divides bbb or bbb divides aaa. Chef wants to identify such a subset that contains the maximum possible number of elements. Help Chef by writing a function that returns any one of these largest divisible subsets sorted in ascending order.

Function DeclarationFunction NamefindLargestDivisibleSubsetfindLargestDivisibleSubsetfindLargestDivisibleSubset - This function finds the largest subset of the given numbers where for every pair (a,b)(a, b)(a,b) in the subset, either aaa divides bbb or bbb divides aaa.

Parameters
inputNumbersinputNumbersinputNumbers: A reference to a vector of integers representing the input set of numbers.
The input contains distinct positive integers with no guaranteed order.
Return Value
Returns a number containing the largest divisible subset.
The returned subset is sorted in ascending order.
The subset represents the maximal collection of numbers where each pair satisfies the divisibility condition.
Constraints
1≤N≤10001 \leq N \leq 10001≤N≤1000
1≤inputNumbers[i]≤2×1091 \leq \text{inputNumbers}[i] \leq 2 \times 10^{9}1≤inputNumbers[i]≤2×109
All integers in inputNumbersinputNumbersinputNumbers are unique.
Input Format
The first line contains a single integer TTT — the number of test cases.
For each test case:

The first line contains an integer NNN — the size of Chef's number array.
The second line contains NNN space-separated integers representing Chef's number array.


Output Format
For each test case, print a single line containing the elements of the largest divisible subset in ascending order, separated by spaces.
If multiple solutions exist, Chef can print any one of them.
constraints: All integers in inputNumbers are unique
content.inputFormat: The first line contains a single integer T, the number of test cases. For each test case: The first line contains an integer N, the size of Chef's number array. The second line contains N space-separated integers representing Chef's number array.
content.outputFormat: For each test case, print a single line containing the elements of the largest divisible subset in ascending order, separated by spaces.

sampleTestCases.explanation: For the first test case, the entire set {1, 3, 6, 24, 36} forms a divisible chain since each number divides the next one. For the second test case, {2, 10, 20} is the largest subset where each element divides the next, excluding 5 which breaks the chain.

For input [1, 2, 3], the largest divisible subset is [1, 2] because 2 is divisible by 1, but 3 is not divisible by 2 or 1. For input [1, 2, 4, 8, 16, 32], all numbers form a chain where each number divides the next, so the entire array is the largest subset. For input [3, 5, 15, 30, 60], the subset [3, 15, 30, 60] is largest since each element divides the next, while 5 breaks the chain.
The input array is already sorted and each number divides the next one (7 divides 14, 14 divides 28, and so on). Hence, the entire array forms the largest divisible subset.

solution.approach: The problem can be solved using dynamic programming. We first sort the input array. Then, for each number in the array, we find the largest divisible subset that ends with this number. We keep track of the length of the longest divisible subset and its previous element. Finally, we construct the largest divisible subset by backtracking from the last element.
solution.timeComplexity: O(N^2) per test case, O(T * N^2) total. The precomputation is done once for each test case.
solution.spaceComplexity: O(N) — the dominant structure is the dynamic programming table.

code.Python3:
import sys

def find_largest_divisible_subset(nums):
    nums.sort()
    dp = [1] * len(nums)
    prev = [-1] * len(nums)
    max_len = 1
    max_idx = 0
    for i in range(1, len(nums)):
        for j in range(i):
            if nums[i] % nums[j] == 0 and dp[i] < dp[j] + 1:
                dp[i] = dp[j] + 1
                prev[i] = j
        if dp[i] > max_len:
            max_len = dp[i]
            max_idx = i
    subset = [0] * max_len
    idx = max_idx
    for i in range(max_len - 1, -1, -1):
        subset[i] = nums[idx]
        idx = prev[idx]
    return subset

T = int(sys.stdin.readline())
for _ in range(T):
    N = int(sys.stdin.readline())
    nums = list(map(int, sys.stdin.readline().split()))
    subset = find_largest_divisible_subset(nums)
    print(' '.join(map(str, subset)))
